a.question: Do all films take on the ZnO wurtzite structure?
a.answer:
A textured polycrystalline layer of nanosized ZnO with a predominant intensity of (100) and (002) index reflections,
which confirms the formation of ZnO with a hexagonal wurtzite structure, is observed for all films.

a.question: How does the XRD pattern vary with doping?
a.answer: While the intensity of reflections with index (100) remains almost the same for all samples, the intensity of (002) reflections varies depending on the doping element. A similar dependence between the intensities of (100) and (002) reflections is also observed for thin films with a low degree of TM doping (with less TM deposition cycles) in literature.

a.question: What reflections are observed with different doping?
a.answer:
1. Pure ZnO: The orientation along the c-axis direction is significantly better expressed, which is proved by the high intensity of reflections with index (002) 
2. Co-ZnO:  Only reflections with index (100) were observed, indicating a change in the orientation
of the ZnO crystallites with an ab-plane perpendicular to the substrate 
3. Ni-ZnO : Both orientations (100) and (002) (ab- and c- oriented respectively) are present
4. Fe-ZnO : The intensity of the (100) peak is substantially larger, that is, it is the preferred orientation. It is also seen that in addition to (100) and (002), a low intensity diagonal reflex (102), which is not observed in pure ZnO, also appears (
